close_reason: Removed OCI imports from precision.scm, replaced with namespace container services. container-home.scm kept (independent guix shell workflow). Podman kept at system level (system/m1.scm).
description:
Cleanup tasks after migration:
- Verify modules/r0man/guix/home/container-home.scm usage — precision.scm uses home-container-home-service-type which may serve the guix home container dev workflow independently of OCI. Only remove if confirmed OCI-only.
- Remove podman from package lists if only included for OCI.
- Verify with dry-run: guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/m1.scm
- Apply and check: herd status gastown-container, herd log gastown-container
- Verify guix shell works inside the container.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Investigate `container-home.scm` usage: it is imported only by `precision.scm` (line 27, 105) and creates a `~/.guix-container-home` symlink used by `files/bin/container-run` script for a `guix shell --container` dev workflow. Document whether this is independent of OCI (it is — it uses `guix shell --container`, not Podman) and keep it if so.
- [ ] Investigate `podman`/`podman-compose` in `packages.scm` (lines 113-114 in `packages-base`): determine if any remaining code references them after OCI removal from m1.scm and precision.scm. If only OCI service types used them, remove from `packages-base`.
- [ ] After removing podman from packages (if applicable), check `system/m1.scm` which has `%rootless-podman-service` — that is system-level config, not home-level, so document that podman may still be needed at the system level even if removed from home packages.
- [ ] Remove OCI-specific imports from `precision.scm`: `(gnu home services containers)`, `(gnu packages containers)`, `(gnu services containers)`, and the `oci-gastown-services` definition, replacing with namespace container services (same pattern as m1.scm migration).
- [ ] Dry-run succeeds for both systems: `guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/m1.scm` and `guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/precision.scm`
- [ ] After applying on at least one system: `herd status gastown-container` shows the service running, and `herd log gastown-container` shows no errors.
title: Cleanup: verify container-home.scm usage and remove podman

close_reason: Replaced OCI section in gastown.scm with namespace container using unshare. All acceptance criteria met.
description:
File: modules/r0man/guix/home/services/gastown.scm — Replace OCI section (lines 225-346).

REMOVE: home-gastown-oci-configuration, gastown-oci-entrypoint, gastown-oci-town-volumes, home-gastown-oci-extension, home-gastown-oci-service-type

REMOVE imports: (gnu home services containers), (gnu services containers), (guix profiles)

ADD import: (gnu packages linux) for util-linux

UPDATE exports: Replace home-gastown-oci-* with home-gastown-container-*

ADD record type:
(define-record-type* <home-gastown-container-configuration>
  home-gastown-container-configuration make-home-gastown-container-configuration
  home-gastown-container-configuration?
  (home-environment home-gastown-container-home-environment)
  (user home-gastown-container-user (default "roman"))
  (towns home-gastown-container-towns (default '()))
  (home-mounts home-gastown-container-home-mounts (default '(".dolt" ".ssh:ro" ".claude"))))

ADD container script (computed-file): Generates bash script run inside unshare. All commands via file-append with full store paths. Uses separate HOME path (/tmp/gastown-home):
1. mount -t tmpfs tmpfs /tmp/gastown-home
2. Create subdirs: mkdir -p /tmp/gastown-home/{gt,.dolt,.ssh,.claude,...}
3. mount --bind /home/roman/gt /tmp/gastown-home/gt (for each shared dir)
4. Read-only remount for :ro entries (mount -o remount,bind,ro)
5. mount -t tmpfs -o mode=700 tmpfs /tmp/gastown-home/.local/run (shepherd socket)
6. mount -t proc proc /proc
7. Bind-mount generated /etc/passwd over host's (maps UID 0 -> username with home /tmp/gastown-home)
8. export HOME=/tmp/gastown-home XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/gastown-home/.local/run
9. Run $HE/activate || exit 1
10. Trap SIGTERM -> herd stop root; kill $SLEEP_PID; exit 0
11. sleep infinity & then SLEEP_PID=$!; wait $SLEEP_PID

ADD shepherd service:
(start #~(make-forkexec-constructor
          (list #$(file-append util-linux "/bin/unshare")
                "--user" "--map-root-user"
                "--mount" "--pid" "--fork" "--kill-child=SIGTERM"
                "--" #$script)
          #:log-file ...))
(stop #~(make-kill-destructor))

ADD host activation: Create town dirs and ~/.dolt so bind-mount sources exist.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No OCI/Podman references remain: `home-gastown-oci-configuration`, `gastown-oci-entrypoint`, `gastown-oci-town-volumes`, `home-gastown-oci-extension`, `home-gastown-oci-service-type` are all deleted from gastown.scm
- [ ] Imports `(gnu home services containers)`, `(gnu services containers)`, and `(guix profiles)` are removed from the module header
- [ ] Import `(gnu packages linux)` is added for `util-linux`
- [ ] Exports list contains `home-gastown-container-configuration` and `home-gastown-container-service-type` (no `oci` exports)
- [ ] `home-gastown-container-configuration` record type is defined with fields: `home-environment`, `user` (default "roman"), `towns` (default '()), `home-mounts` (default '(".dolt" ".ssh:ro" ".claude"))
- [ ] A `computed-file` generates a bash script that: mounts tmpfs on /tmp/gastown-home, bind-mounts configured home dirs (respecting :ro suffix), mounts /proc, bind-mounts a generated /etc/passwd, sets HOME and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, runs activation, traps SIGTERM for graceful shutdown, and sleeps to keep alive
- [ ] All commands in the container script use `file-append` with full store paths (no bare `mount`, `mkdir`, etc.)
- [ ] `home-gastown-container-service-type` is defined with a shepherd extension whose start uses `unshare --user --map-root-user --mount --pid --fork --kill-child=SIGTERM` and stop uses `make-kill-destructor`
- [ ] Host activation creates town directories and ~/.dolt so bind-mount sources exist before the container starts
- [ ] The existing `home-gastown-service-type` (non-OCI, lines 1-222) is preserved unchanged
- [ ] `guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/m1.scm` succeeds without errors (after m1.scm is updated in the dependent task)
title: Replace OCI service with namespace container in gastown.scm

description: home-gastown-container-configuration supports home-mounts but lacks env var pass-through and host socket access. Add environment-variables field and map-host-runtime-dir? field to enable host resource access from the container.
title: Configurable paths and env vars for gastown container

close_reason: All 4 subtasks complete. OCI/Podman replaced with unshare namespace container across gastown.scm, gastown HE, m1.scm, and precision.scm. m1 dry-run succeeds.
description: Replace the heavyweight Podman/OCI gastown container service (home-gastown-oci-service-type) with a lightweight Linux namespace container using unshare. A computed-file bash script handles namespace setup, mounts, and activation. The host shepherd runs it via unshare --user --map-root-user --mount --pid --fork --kill-child=SIGTERM. No Podman dependency. Key design: separate HOME path (/tmp/gastown-home), no pivot_root so host filesystem stays visible (guix shell works), --map-root-user for mount operations, shared host network, --kill-child=SIGTERM for graceful shutdown.
title: Replace OCI/Podman gastown container with Linux namespace container

close_reason: Removed OCI/Podman imports and replaced oci-gastown-services with gastown-container-services in m1.scm. Dry-run succeeds.
description:
File: modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/m1.scm

- Remove imports: (gnu home services containers), (gnu packages containers), (gnu services containers)
- Replace oci-gastown-services with:
  (define gastown-container-services
    (list (service home-gastown-container-service-type
                   (home-gastown-container-configuration
                    (home-environment gastown-he)
                    (user "roman")
                    (towns gastown-towns)))))
- Update (services (append services gastown-container-services))
- No more home-oci-service-type / Podman setup

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Imports `(gnu home services containers)`, `(gnu packages containers)`, and `(gnu services containers)` are removed from m1.scm
- [ ] Import `(r0man guix home services gastown)` is present (already exists, provides `home-gastown-container-service-type`)
- [ ] The `oci-gastown-services` definition is replaced by `gastown-container-services` using `home-gastown-container-service-type` with `home-gastown-container-configuration`
- [ ] The `gastown-container-services` configuration passes `gastown-he` as `home-environment`, `"roman"` as `user`, and `gastown-towns` as `towns`
- [ ] No `home-oci-service-type` or `oci-configuration` or Podman references remain in m1.scm
- [ ] The `m1-home-environment` services append uses `gastown-container-services` instead of `oci-gastown-services`
- [ ] `guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/m1.scm` completes without errors
- [ ] After `guix home reconfigure`, `herd status gastown-container` shows the service exists and is running
- [ ] The gastown container process tree shows `unshare` as the top-level process (not podman)
title: Update m1.scm to use namespace container service

close_reason: Updated commentary, added guix package import and to packages list.
description:
File: modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/gastown.scm

- Update commentary ('OCI' -> 'namespace container')
- Add guix to the packages in make-gastown-home-environment so guix shell works inside the container:
  (packages (append (list bash coreutils git guix nss-certs) extra-packages))
- The guix package is needed because without pivot_root the host filesystem is visible including /gnu/store and /var/guix/daemon-socket/socket, so guix shell can work if the guix binary is available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Commentary in gastown.scm says "namespace container" instead of "OCI containers"
- [ ] `(gnu packages package-management)` is added to `#:use-module` imports (provides the `guix` package)
- [ ] `guix` is added to the packages list: `(packages (append (list bash coreutils git guix nss-certs) extra-packages))`
- [ ] `guix home -L modules build modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/gastown.scm` succeeds (module loads and the home-environment builds without errors)
- [ ] The built home-environment profile contains the `guix` binary (verify with `ls $(guix home -L modules build modules/r0man/guix/home/systems/gastown.scm)/profile/bin/guix`)
title: Update gastown home-environment definition
